ui.navDisabled: Scene Navigation is disabled in the 'Elements' tab. The settings below will only have effect if Custom Layout is enabled for Scene Navigation.
ui.reloadRequiredLabel: A reload is required. Reload now?
ui.enforcementTooltip: Click to cycle lock state. Right-click (or Alt+Click) to toggle GM mode

notifications.incompatibleVersion: Carolingian UI requires Foundry v13.339 or higher. Please update Foundry or install version 1.x of Carolingian UI.
notifications.settingsSavedAsDefaults: Current settings saved as defaults for players
notifications.enforceGMSettingsConflict: Carolingian UI and Force Client Settings module are both enforcing settings. This may cause confusing behavior.
notifications.minimalUiNotSupported: Carolingian UI is not compatible with Minimal UI, and offers most of its features. Please disable that module to avoid performance issues.
notifications.monksScenesNotSupported: Carolingian UI's Scene Navigation and Monk's Scene Navigation are NOT compatible. Please either disable Monk's Scene Navigation or uncheck 'Custom Layout' for Scene Navigation in settings.
notifications.ripperScenesCompat: Carolingian UI now has Scene Folder Navigation and Scene Search, which cannot be used together with Compact Scene Navigation. Either disable the other module or disable 'Scene Folder Navigation' on Carolingian UI settings.
notifications.monksPlayerSettingsConflict: Carolingian UI's setting 'Enforce Dark Mode' has been disabled to prevent conflict with the module Monk's Player Settings.
notifications.forceClientSettingsConflict: Carolingian UI's setting 'Enforce Dark Mode' has been disabled to prevent conflict with Force Client Settings.
notifications.initialViewSet: This scene's initial view was set to the current view.

updates.label: Last Update ID
updates.hint: Tracks the last displayed update notification

carouselWelcome.title: New feature: Combat Encounter Carousel
carouselWelcome.message: Carolingian UI has a new draggable and dockable Combat Encounter Carousel. Would you like to enable this feature?
carouselWelcome.enabledNotification: Combat Carousel has been enabled. You can change this in module settings.
carouselWelcome.disabledNotification: Combat Carousel has been disabled. You can enable it later in module settings.
carouselWelcome.moduleWarning: Warning: The Combat Carousel module is currently active. Consider disabling it to avoid conflicts.

supportPatreon.label: Support the developer
supportPatreon.hint: If you like Carolingian UI and my other modules, consider supporting the development with a few bucks per month.

exportSettings.label: Troubleshooting
exportSettings.hint: Export your Carolingian UI settings and active modules to a JSON file for troubleshooting.

importSettings.label: Import Settings
importSettings.hint: Import Carolingian UI settings from a JSON file. GM only.
importSettings.invalidFile: Invalid settings file. Must contain cuiSettings or carolingianSettings.
importSettings.parseError: Failed to parse settings file. Please check the file format.
importSettings.reloadPrompt: Settings have been imported. Reload the page to apply all changes?

bulkLockAction.label: Bulk Lock Settings (GM)
bulkLockAction.hint: Apply a lock state to all player settings at once. This is a one-time action.
bulkLockAction.confirmMessage: Are you sure you want to set ALL player settings to '{state}'? This will overwrite all individual lock states.
bulkLockAction.success: Applied the state to all client settings: '{state}'

moduleSettingsMenu.label: Interface Settings
moduleSettingsMenu.title: Configure Options
moduleSettingsMenu.hint: Customize Carolingian UI, enable or disable custom styles and other options.

hideInterface.label: Toggle Hide/Show Interface
hideInterface.hint: Hides or shows the UI. This will affect all elements inside the `#interface` html block

toggleSidebar.label: Sidebar Expand/Collapse
toggleSidebar.hint: Expands or collapses the right sidebar

combatPrev.label: Combat: Previous Turn
combatPrev.hint: Go to the previous turn in combat. Only works when you own the current combatant.

combatNext.label: Combat: Next Turn
combatNext.hint: Advance to the next turn in combat. Only works when you own the current combatant.

interfaceOptionsMenu.label: Fade-out & Layout
interfaceOptionsMenu.title: Interface Fade-out & Layout
interfaceOptionsMenu.hint: Disable 'fade out' behavior of interface elements individually. You may also disable Carolingian UI custom layout for them.
interfaceOptionsMenu.description: 'Fade Out' column: Uncheck to prevent element from fading out when not hovered. 'Custom Layout' column: Uncheck to disable Carolingian UI styles for that particular element. Color theming and fonts will still apply.

hoverableSettingsHints.label: Hoverable Settings Hints
hoverableSettingsHints.hint: This cleans up settings and application forms by adding question mark tooltips to show/hide hint text on hover. Affects default Foundry settings and module windows.

preventMacroBarReposition.label: Prevent Macro Bar Reposition
preventMacroBarReposition.hint: When enabled, hiding the chat input box also prevents the macro hotbar from being repositioned when sidebar tab changes. Keeps the macro hotbar in its original position regardless of sidebar state.

enableCombatTrackerCarousel.label: Use Draggable Combat Carousel
enableCombatTrackerCarousel.hint: When enabled, the combat tracker will automatically pop out when combat has combatants and close when combat ends. Also enables custom styling for the popped out tracker.

combatCarouselScale.label: Docked Carousel Scale
combatCarouselScale.hint: Scale of the combat carousel when docked to the top of the screen. Range: 0.7 (70%) to 1.5 (150%).

dockedCarouselOffset.label: Docked Carousel Offset
dockedCarouselOffset.hint: Offset in pixels when the carousel is docked. Applies as margin-top when docked to the top, or margin-bottom when docked to the bottom.

carouselDockOnLoad.label: Dock Carousel on Load
carouselDockOnLoad.hint: Controls where the carousel docks when Foundry loads.

combatTrackerTakeFullWidth.label: Carousel Responds to Sidebar
combatTrackerTakeFullWidth.hint: When enabled, the carousel takes full available width if necessary when the sidebar is collapsed, and adjusts back when the sidebar is expanded.

carouselImageSource.label: Carousel Image Source
carouselImageSource.hint: Choose whether to display token images or actor/character portraits in the combat carousel.

combatTrackerLayout.label: Combat Tracker Layout
combatTrackerLayout.hint: Choose how the combat tracker behaves. Carousel mode centers on the active combatant and wraps around. Simple list mode is left-aligned and scrolls to show the active combatant when needed.

carouselShowAllHP.label: Resource Bar Visibility
carouselShowAllHP.hint: Controls who can see resource/HP bars on combatants. Requires a Tracked Resource to be configured. This is a world setting (GM).

carouselTrackedResource.label: Tracked Resource
carouselTrackedResource.hint: The resource attribute to display on combatant cards (e.g. HP). This is a world setting (GM).

carouselHideDefeated.label: Hide Defeated Combatants
carouselHideDefeated.hint: When enabled, defeated combatants are removed from the carousel so remaining positions are recalculated.

carouselRequirePlayerOwner.label: Require Player Character
carouselRequirePlayerOwner.hint: When enabled, the carousel only opens automatically when a player character is added to combat. When disabled, it opens for any combatant.

customFontsMenu.label: Fonts
customFontsMenu.title: Configure Fonts
customFontsMenu.hint: Configure the font families used throughout the interface
customFontsMenu.description: Select or type the name of a custom font, if already loaded on Foundry core.

enableFontUI.label: Enable UI Font
enableFontUI.hint: Apply custom font to UI body text

enableFontTitles.label: Enable Titles Font
enableFontTitles.hint: Apply custom font to UI headings

enableFontJournal.label: Enable Journal Font
enableFontJournal.hint: Apply custom font to journal body text

enableFontJournalTitles.label: Enable Journal Titles Font
enableFontJournalTitles.hint: Apply custom font to journal headings

customFontsMenu.postDescription: To import from Google Fonts, first paste the @import link in the "Custom CSS" field of style settings, and the font will be listed here.

themeAndStylesMenu.label: Theme
themeAndStylesMenu.title: Configure Styles
themeAndStylesMenu.hint: Select a color theme for the UI, and add custom CSS styles.

colorTheme.label: Color Theme (World)
colorTheme.hint: Select an option for the color highlights and secondary color. Only affects highlights, inputs and buttons. Player color theme can override this setting.

playerColorTheme.label: Color Theme (Player)
playerColorTheme.hint: Select an option for the color highlights and secondary color. Only affects highlights, inputs and buttons

customThemeColors.label: Custom Theme Colors (World)
customThemeColors.hint: Custom color configuration for the world theme

playerCustomThemeColors.label: Custom Theme Colors (Player)
playerCustomThemeColors.hint: Custom color configuration for this player

useGlassEffect.label: Enable Glass Effect (may affect performance)
useGlassEffect.hint: Enable backdrop-filter glass effect on application windows. This feature can cause performance issues on slower machines. Disable if you experience lag or stuttering, and backdrop-filter will not be applied at all.

glassTranslucence.label: Glass Translucence
glassTranslucence.hint: Controls the translucence of windows. 0 = fully opaque with minimal blur, 1 = highly translucent with maximum blur. Opacity and blur adjust inversely for readability. Control is disabled on low performance mode.

enableJournalStyles.label: Apply styles to journal sheets
enableJournalStyles.hint: Enable to apply Carolingian UI styling to journal entry sheets. Disable if third-party journal modules have styling conflicts.

fields.customizeHint: Click to open the color picker and create custom theme colors

adjustOtherModules.label: Enforce styles in other modules
adjustOtherModules.hint: Enable Carolingian UI style tweaks on other modules. If you would like support for a specific module, please request on Github issues

applyDarkThemeToModules.label: Apply Dark Theme to Application Windows
applyDarkThemeToModules.hint: Some modules/systems never implemented dark theme. This option attempts to apply dark theme to application windows using .app.theme-light class. If some window becomes partially illegible, uncheck this option.

otherModulesList.label: Enforce styles in other modules
otherModulesList.hint: Enable Carolingian UI style tweaks on other modules. Mark each desired checkbox or the one on top of the list to select / unselect all. If you are having issues with one module, unmark its checkbox

forcedDarkTheme.label: Force Dark Theme via CSS
forcedDarkTheme.hint: EXPERIMENTAL. If the above checkbox doesn't work for a specific module, you may attempt to force dark background and light font to modules by css selector. It might not always work if modules have hardcoded styles. Enter in the field below the css id or class of the elements, separated by comma. Example: #element-id, .element-class

customStyles.label: Custom CSS
customStyles.hint: These styles will be loaded the next time you load Foundry. You may include Google Fonts by pasting the @import link, and the font will be listed in the Custom Fonts settings.

enforceDarkMode.label: Enforce Dark Mode to Players
enforceDarkMode.hint: Do not enable this if using Force Client Settings or similar modules. This sets Foundry's UI theme on player's client to Dark if they have it set to 'Browser Default'. If they specifically set it to 'Light', their choice will be respected.

sidebarMenu.label: Chat
sidebarMenu.title: Configure Chat
sidebarMenu.hint: Modify the look and behavior of the chat message cards on right side panel

enableChatStyles.label: Enable styles for chat messages
enableChatStyles.hint: Adds style modifications to chat cards. When dark mode is selected on Foundry, chat cards will be dark as well. MOSTLY FOR DND5E - only partial support for other systems.

borderColor.label: Color of chat card border
borderColor.hint: [Requires reload] Player color: uses the player's color on Foundry; Roll Type: uses color coding for attack, save, skill check, etc. None: no border at all (will allow borders set by other modules).

borderPosition.label: Position of colored border
borderPosition.hint:
[Requires reload] Left: border shows up only on the left side of the chat card.
Top: border shows up only on the top side of the chat card.
None: no border at all (will allow borders set by other modules).
All: border shows up on all sides of the chat card.

useLeftChatBorder.label: Use left border only
useLeftChatBorder.hint: Your border color choice shows up on the left side of the chat card only - the other sides have no border.

sideBarWidth.label: Sidebar width
sideBarWidth.hint: Width of the sidebar panel on the right side of the screen.

openChatLogOnLoad.label: Open Chat Log on Load
openChatLogOnLoad.hint: When enabled, the sidebar will automatically expand to show the Chat Log when the module loads.

closeSidebarWhenIdle.label: Close Sidebar When Idle
closeSidebarWhenIdle.hint: When enabled, the sidebar will automatically collapse 3 seconds after your mouse leaves the sidebar area.

useHorizontalSidebarTabs.label: Use Horizontal Sidebar Tabs
useHorizontalSidebarTabs.hint: When enabled, changes the sidebar tabs to horizontal orientation, at the top of the sidebar, instead of vertically on the side. (v12 style)

showChatNotificationsOnTop.label: Show Chat Notifications From the Top
showChatNotificationsOnTop.hint: When enabled, chat card notifications appear from the top of the chat input area instead of the bottom.

useFolderStyle.label: Discreet folder colors on sidebar
useFolderStyle.hint: Enable more discreet colors on sidebar directory folders, showing the selected color only on the borders, for a nicer look.

hiddenSidebarTabs.label: Sidebar Tabs Visibility & Order
hiddenSidebarTabs.hint: Control which sidebar tabs are visible for GMs and players. Hidden tabs will not appear in the sidebar.

leftControlsMenu.label: Control Bar
leftControlsMenu.title: Configure Controls
leftControlsMenu.hint: Modify the look and behavior of scene control buttons, on the left side of screen

autoHideSecondary.label: Auto hide secondary controls
autoHideSecondary.hint: Enable to keep the secondary bar on the left controls hidden until you hover on the region or click on an item.

playersListMenu.label: Players List
playersListMenu.title: Player List Options
playersListMenu.hint: Change behavior or add avatars to players list

autoHidePlayerList.label: Auto hide player list
autoHidePlayerList.hint: Enable to keep the players list on bottom left minimized, except when hovered.

playerListAvatars.label: Show avatars
playerListAvatars.hint: Show player avatars for players in the list. It looks for character image, or user image if no character is assigned

playerListAvatarSize.label: Avatar size
playerListAvatarSize.hint: Size of player avatars in the list.

cameraDockMenu.label: Camera Group
cameraDockMenu.title: Configure Camera Group
cameraDockMenu.hint: Enable a floating camera group that you can position anywhere, dock and resize

enableFloatingDock.label: Enable floating camera group
enableFloatingDock.hint: [Requires reload] Allows video player group to be dragged and placed anywhere on screen

dockPosX.label: Horizontal position of camera group
dockPosX.hint: Position of camera group when set to float. From bottom left of screen, in pixels

dockPosY.label: Vertical position of camera group
dockPosY.hint: Position of camera group when set to float. From bottom left of screen, in pixels

dockWidth.label: Width of camera group
dockWidth.hint: Dimensions of the non-minimized camera group when set to float, in pixels

dockHeight.label: Height of camera group
dockHeight.hint: Dimensions of the non-minimized camera dock when set to float, in pixels

dockCamerasToBottom.label: Allow docking cameras to macro hotbar
dockCamerasToBottom.hint: When enabled, you can drag the camera group to the bottom of the screen (above the macro hotbar) to dock it. When docked, the camera group will be horizontal, span the full available width and shrink videos to a minimum of 150px. Drag it away from the bottom to undock.

dockResizeOnUserJoin.label: Grow Dock to Fit Videos
dockResizeOnUserJoin.hint: When enabled, the dock will be resized when a user joins so new videos are visible. Any manual resize may be overriden when a new user joins. To keep manual resizing and scrolling, turn this option off.

defaultVideoWidth.label: Default video width
defaultVideoWidth.hint: Width of each individual camera in dock, in pixels. Minimum width is 150px, to allow space for camera control icons.

combatTrackerMenu.label: Combat Tracker
combatTrackerMenu.title: Configure Combat Tracker
combatTrackerMenu.hint: Configure the combat tracker carousel appearance and behavior.

enableCombatTrackerCarousel.label: Use Draggable Combat Carousel
enableCombatTrackerCarousel.hint: When enabled, the combat tracker will automatically pop out when combat has combatants and close when combat ends. Also enables custom styling for the popped out tracker.

autoPopoutOnCombatStart.label: Auto Pop Out Combat Tracker
autoPopoutOnCombatStart.hint: When enabled, the combat tracker pops out automatically when a combat is started or combatants are added. Turn off if you'd rather open it manually.

combatCarouselScale.label: Docked Carousel Scale
combatCarouselScale.hint: Scale of the combat carousel when docked to the top of the screen. Range: 0.7 (70%) to 1.5 (150%).

dockedCarouselOffset.label: Docked Carousel Offset
dockedCarouselOffset.hint: Offset in pixels when the carousel is docked. Applies as margin-top when docked to the top, or margin-bottom when docked to the bottom.

carouselDockOnLoad.label: Dock Carousel on Load
carouselDockOnLoad.hint: Controls where the carousel docks when Foundry loads.

combatTrackerTakeFullWidth.label: Carousel Responds to Sidebar
combatTrackerTakeFullWidth.hint: When enabled, the carousel takes full available width if necessary when the sidebar is collapsed, and adjusts back when the sidebar is expanded.

carouselImageSource.label: Carousel Image Source
carouselImageSource.hint: Choose whether to display token images or actor/character portraits in the combat carousel.

combatTrackerLayout.label: Combat Tracker Layout
combatTrackerLayout.hint: Choose how the combat tracker behaves. Carousel mode centers on the active combatant and wraps around. Simple list mode is left-aligned and scrolls to show the active combatant when needed.

carouselHideDefeated.label: Hide Defeated Combatants
carouselHideDefeated.hint: When enabled, defeated combatants are removed from the carousel so remaining positions are recalculated.

carouselRequirePlayerOwner.label: Require Player Character
carouselRequirePlayerOwner.hint: When enabled, the carousel only opens automatically when a player character is added to combat. When disabled, it opens for any combatant.

carouselShowAllHP.label: Resource Bar Visibility
carouselShowAllHP.hint: Controls who can see resource/HP bars on combatants. Requires a Tracked Resource to be configured. This is a world setting (GM).

carouselTrackedResource.label: Tracked Resource
carouselTrackedResource.hint: The resource attribute to display on combatant cards (e.g. HP). This is a world setting (GM).

showCombatRoundButtons.label: Show Round Navigation Buttons
showCombatRoundButtons.hint: When enabled, Previous Round and Next Round buttons are visible in the combat controls. This is a world setting (GM).

autoRollNPCsOnCombatStart.label: Auto-roll for NPCs when starting combat
autoRollNPCsOnCombatStart.hint: When enabled, NPC initiative is rolled automatically as soon as combat starts, skipping the roll dialog. This is a world setting (GM).

sceneNavMenu.label: Scene Nav
sceneNavMenu.title: Configure Scene Nav
sceneNavMenu.hint: Change options for the scene navigation menu, on the top bar.

hideLoadingSceneName.label: Hide scene name from loading bar
hideLoadingSceneName.hint: The loading bar shows the scene name, which can spoil content for players. Check this option to show only a thin loading bar instead.

disableActiveSceneSeparation.label: Disable active/inactive scene separation
disableActiveSceneSeparation.hint: When enabled, active scenes will be displayed in the same list as inactive scenes instead of being separated at the top.

sceneNavEnabled.label: Enable custom scene navigation
sceneNavEnabled.hint: [Requires reload] If you are having layout issues with a non-compatible module, you can try to disable my custom styles for Scene Navigation.

showNavOnHover.label: Toggle scene navigation on hover
showNavOnHover.hint: If the scene navigation is collapsed, you can show it by hovering the mouse over the top of the screen.

useSceneFolders.label: Enable scene folders on navigation
useSceneFolders.hint: If custom scene navigation is enabled, this option will add a list of folders with scene search to scene navigation bar.

useSceneLookup.label: Enable scene search on navigation
useSceneLookup.hint: If custom scene navigation is enabled, this option will add a button to search for scenes on the navigation bar.

navFoldersForPlayers.label: Enable scene folders for players
navFoldersForPlayers.hint: Enable to allow players to see the folder list. If they do not have view permissions for a folder, it will not appear on the list.

navStartCollapsed.label: Start collapsed
navStartCollapsed.hint: Enable this option to collapse the scene navigation when loading Foundry, regardless of previous saved state.

collapseNavDuringCombat.label: Collapse during combat
collapseNavDuringCombat.hint: Automatically collapse the scene navigation when combat starts. The navigation will restore to its previous state when combat ends.

sceneClickToView.label: Enable Click/Double Click to View/Activate
sceneClickToView.hint: When enabled, clicking on the scene in scene once opens that scene. Double clicking activates it.

useSceneIcons.label: Use scene icons
useSceneIcons.hint: Shows icons next to the scene name. Invisible to players until activated; Currently viewed by the GM; Currently active to players;

useSceneBackButton.label: Enable Back Button
useSceneBackButton.hint: Add a back button to scene navigation, which lets you quick change back to previous scene viewed

useScenePreview.label: Use Scene Preview
useScenePreview.hint: When enabled, the thumbnail preview will be shown when hovering over scene navigation

navShowRootFolders.label: Show root folders in scene navigation
navShowRootFolders.hint: Root folders from Scene Directory will be show in the default scene navigation. If you have many folders on root, you might want to disable this option.

hideInactiveOnFolderToggle.label: Hide inactive scenes on folder toggle
hideInactiveOnFolderToggle.hint: When enabled, default list of scenes will be hidden when folder view is on. The list will show up again when you hide folders. This aims to avoid duplication of scenes in navigation.

subFoldersLayout.label: Subfolder row alignment
subFoldersLayout.hint: Choose how scenes inside subfolders are positioned when the folder is expanded. Only works if folders are enabled.

expandScrimToSubfolders.label: Expand background behind subfolders
expandScrimToSubfolders.hint: When enabled, the navigation background expands to cover subfolder rows when folders are open.

autoCloseSubmenuOnSceneChange.label: Auto-close submenu on scene change
autoCloseSubmenuOnSceneChange.hint: When enabled, any open folder submenu closes after a scene is selected or activated. When disabled, folders stay open until manually closed.

sceneItemWidth.label: Width of navigation item
sceneItemWidth.hint: Leave empty to use default width, or define a new number. Minimum is 100px

systemsMenu.label: System
systemsMenu.title: Configure options for specific systems
systemsMenu.noSettingsForSystem: No specific settings for the current system

applyThemeToSheets.label: Apply theme to actor sheets
applyThemeToSheets.hint: Enable to apply global color theme and styles to actor sheets (PC and NPC)

useHorizontalSheetTabs.label: Use horizontal tabs with labels on sheets
useHorizontalSheetTabs.hint: Only works when custom styles for sheets are enabled. This applies horizontal tabs with labels on 5e actor sheets instead of the lateral icon tabs

enableIconsOnSheets.label: Enable chat and tooltip icons on sheets
enableIconsOnSheets.hint: Show 'Display in Chat' overlay on item images and scroll icons for item tooltips on character sheets

applyBladeRunnerTweaks.label: Apply UI tweaks to Blade Runner system
applyBladeRunnerTweaks.hint: Enable to apply UI adjustments and style tweaks to Blade Runner sheets and windows

enforceGMSettings.label: Enforce GM Settings to Players
enforceGMSettings.hint: When enabled, all players will use the GM's UI settings when they connect. This ensures a consistent UI experience across all players.

defaultSettings.label: Default GM Settings
defaultSettings.hint: Stores the GM's preferred settings for enforcement to players

controlIconSize.label: Size of control icons
controlIconSize.hint: If you use a large resolution, you may want to change the size of the icons on left controls. This may affect some other UI elements

sceneNavCollapsed.label: Scene navigation collapsed
sceneNavCollapsed.hint: Current state of the scene navigation toggle. Is it collapsed?

sceneNavPos.label: Scene navigation position
sceneNavPos.hint: Stores current position of navigation for reset when it's re-rendered

enableMacroLayout.label: Enable macro layout
enableMacroLayout.hint: The module enlarges and centralizes numbers on macro hotbar. Disable this setting to use default Foundry layout.

autoHideLeftControls.label: Auto hide secondary controls
autoHideLeftControls.hint: Enable to keep the secondary bar on the left controls hidden unless you hover on the region or click on an item.

collapseMacroBar.label: Auto-Hide Macro Bar
collapseMacroBar.hint: Enable to have the macro hotbar start collapsed by default, and show up on hover.

debugMode.label: Debug Mode
debugMode.hint: Enable or disable debug messages on browser console. This should be turned off unless the module developer asks you to turn it on for verification.

disableUI.label: Disable UI in this client
disableUI.hint: If a player doesn't want to use Carolingian UI, they may disable it. No other feature of the module will work except for dark style in chat cards - which can be separately disabled.

dockDHResources.label: Dock Daggerheart Fear Tracker
dockDHResources.hint: Enable docking functionality for the Daggerheart resource (fear) tracker. Drag the window near the macro hotbar to dock it at the bottom of the screen.

colorPicker.hint: Remember to care for your retinas: for the accent color, use colors with at least a 'medium' contrast rating for better readability.
colorPicker.title: Custom Theme Colors

applySecondaryColorToBg.label: Apply Secondary color to Foundry interface backgrounds
applySecondaryColorToBg.hint: When enabled, the secondary color will be applied to interface backgrounds like sidebar and controls. This allows you to control background of applications in color theme.

actorOverrides.title: Per-Class / Per-Race Accent (DnD5e)
actorOverrides.hint: Override the accent color on individual character sheets based on the character's highest-level class OR their race. Leave a row's color empty to fall back to the theme accent.
actorOverrides.perActorHint: Set an accent color just for this character. Overrides class/race rules and the global theme. Use the button below to clear the override and fall back to those.

combat.closedNotice: When you want the carousel back, right-click the combat tab on sidebar
combat.noTrackedResource: Combat Encounter is set to show resource bar for all charcters, but no tracked resource in Core Foundry is configured in Combat Tracker settings.
combat.configureResourceMessage: The carousel is set to show resource/HP bars for all combatants, but no tracked resource is configured. Select a resource to track below:
